chunk_summary: Guidelines for outpatient energy prescriptions to address growth faltering and catch-up growth in children, including energy gap diagnosis, practical energy targets, food-based densification, and monitoring strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the steps to diagnose and address energy gaps in children with growth faltering.
Learn practical strategies for food-based energy densification in low-resource settings.
Recognize when to refer children with severe malnutrition for inpatient care.
Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of outpatient energy prescriptions.
key_facts: Energy targets for catch-up growth should include 10–20% more calories for mild wasting and higher for severe wasting.
Use MUAC and clinical risk factors (e.g., recent diarrhea, poor appetite) to determine urgency of intervention.
Food-based densification strategies include adding 1–2 teaspoons of oil/ghee, groundnut paste, or protein-rich foods like egg, fish, or dal.
Recheck weight in 2–4 weeks; expect upward crossing of percentiles or flattening of weight loss.
Children with weight-for-length/height < -3 SD or edema should be referred urgently for inpatient care.
reference_quote: Start around the age/sex requirement from ICMR-NIN and add a structured increment for catch-up (often 10–20% more for mild wasting and higher for severe wasting).
document_key_topics: Energy gap diagnosis in growth faltering
Practical energy targets for catch-up growth
Monitoring and referral for severe malnutrition
